message:
fix(malloc): track bump-pointer allocations in fallback insert

Removes the is_bump_ptr() short-circuit from fallback_insert_sized()
so allocations from the bump arena get a fallback length entry just
like heap allocations. Without this, known_remaining() on a
bump-pointer returns None, and the read/write tracked-region guard
in unistd_abi treats the buffer as untracked — letting through
exactly the EFAULT-able transfers it was meant to catch.

The is_bump_ptr exclusion predates the tracked-region work; it was
correct when the only consumer was free(), since freeing a bump
pointer is a no-op. With known_remaining now driving an EFAULT
gate, every tracked allocation needs to be queryable, bump-arena
included.

Side-helper:
* New malloc_known_remaining_for_tests() exposes known_remaining
  through a #[doc(hidden)] surface so test crates can assert the
  pre-call invariant ("allocation is tracked as shorter than the
  requested transfer") before exercising the read/write
  rejection path.

Tests:
* read_rejects_tracked_short_output_buffer and
  write_rejects_tracked_short_input_buffer now assert
  malloc_known_remaining_for_tests(raw) < OVERLONG_IO_LEN before
  the call. If a future regression untracks the bump pointer
  again, the new assertion fails before the syscall path with a
  diagnostic message rather than the test silently changing
  behavior to pass through.
